test.observation: Vitest exited 1 with exactly 9 known pre-existing failures confined to src/db/__tests__/schema.test.ts (8) and src/app/api/health-data/__tests__/route.test.ts (1), while src/db/__tests__/audit-immutability.test.ts remained fully skipped; no new failing test files were introduced.

typecheck.observation: tsc --noEmit completed successfully with exit code 0.

lint.observation: next lint completed with no ESLint warnings or errors.

blockingIssues.description: NightDetailView expects baseline keys aligned to intraday strip metrics (glucose/heart_rate), but useNightView(date) defaults to daily metrics, so required baseline bands are absent in normal usage.
Recovery page hard-codes a 5-day range via subDays(endDate, 4) and does not provide the required selectable 3–7 day range control.
Trend chart uses new Date(YYYY-MM-DD).getTime() for date-only strings, causing UTC parsing day shifts for non-UTC users and incorrect date plotting.
Dashboard navigation uses router.replace for view/date transitions, overwriting history and breaking browser-back context preservation required by VAL-CROSS-009.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a required verification check that NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL matches the running local web port before agent-browser validation begins.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: night-detail-view-page handoff shows browser validation failed with NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000 while .factory/services.yaml runs web on 3100, despite healthy local endpoints.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a frontend date parsing convention: never parse date-only API values with new Date('YYYY-MM-DD'); use local-safe parsing (for example parseISO plus local normalization) for chart domains.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: trend-view-page review found a blocking timezone day-shift bug from new Date(d.date).getTime() in core chart rendering.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require workers to preserve assertion semantics when features are mapped to validation contract IDs (for example, browser-back behavior for VAL-CROSS-009) and explicitly avoid substituting alternative UX without contract alignment.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: dashboard-routing-and-navigation review found replace-based navigation rationale in transcript despite VAL-CROSS-009 requiring back-navigation context preservation.
